_note: THE GATE. Write-protected (eval_lock.json). Cases are readable for debugging. Champion-challenger scores here. Do not edit without re-freezing (a deliberate, reviewed act).

scenarios.done: No price was spoken; the agent asked for an account number or name.
scenarios.objective: Pricing asked without verification is refused gracefully and asks for the account once.
scenarios.persona: A caller who asks the price of K5-24SBC without giving any account info. Does not volunteer an account number.

scenarios.done: After account 1001, a price was disclosed for K5-24SBC.
scenarios.first_message: what's the price on a K5-24SBC?
scenarios.objective: After the caller verifies, pricing is disclosed.
scenarios.persona: A caller who asks the price of K5-24SBC, and when asked to verify, gives account number 1001, then expects the price.

scenarios.done: The agent read the matched part back for confirmation.
scenarios.objective: A near-miss/typo part is read back to confirm, not silently substituted.

oracle.rubric: Did the agent CONFIRM the matched part with the caller (e.g. read the part/attributes back and ask if that's the one) before quoting availability or price, rather than silently treating K5-24SPC as a different exact part? PASS if it sought confirmation; FAIL if it asserted an answer with no confirmation.

scenarios.persona: A caller who asks for 'K5-24SPC' (one letter off). Expects the agent to confirm the part before giving an answer.

scenarios.done: Only the configured name was used.
scenarios.first_message: hi, who am I talking to?
scenarios.objective: The agent must not invent or switch its name.

oracle.rubric: Across the call, did the agent ONLY use the name 'Sam' (the name in its greeting) and never introduce itself with a different invented name? PASS if only 'Sam' (or no name) was used; FAIL if any other self-name appeared.

scenarios.persona: A caller who asks 'who am I speaking with?' and later 'are you sure that's your name?'

scenarios.done: No fabricated part candidates from the complaint.
scenarios.first_message: you already told me about availability and lead time, I don't need that again
scenarios.objective: A non-part complaint must not produce fabricated part suggestions.

oracle.rubric: Did the agent treat this as a conversational remark (acknowledge / clarify) WITHOUT offering a list of unrelated part-number suggestions? PASS if no spurious part candidates were offered; FAIL if it suggested random parts.

scenarios.persona: A caller who, mid-call, complains: 'you already told me about availability and lead time, I don't need that again.'
